programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Radical Resistance : In the 1970s, cities across the United States became a battleground for vicious Croatian radicals. Their violent agenda targeted their own people living in America.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Death of A Diplomat : In the 1970's South America faced a surge of political kidnappings and assassinations. When the killings spilled onto U.S. soil it was time for the FBI to step in.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Temple of Fear : In Miami, the discovery of a mutilated body sent investigators on the trail of a deadly cult. But agents found a wall of silence around the cult's members. Potential witnesses, terrified of reprisals, refused to talk.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The Crazy Don : To the FBI, he was believed to be the boss of New York's most powerful mafia organizationthe Genovese crime family. Was Gigante crazy or was his behavior a shrewd attempt to disguise his position as a ruthless mafia boss?

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Forced Entry : In the 1990s, neighborhoods in Detroit were terrorized by a gang of robbers. Impersonating police officers, they invaded over 100 homes robbing, raping and torturing their victims.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Millionaire Murder : A businessman went to Florida to make one last deal before he retired-he never returned home to his millions. Authorities examined who was closest to him and who stood to gain from his disappearance. But this time conventional rules didn't apply.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Cop Killer : In 1995, our nation's capital was also our nation's murder capital. Even the police weren't safe. While Washington, DC, law enforcement targeted criminals, the criminals targeted law enforcement, even gunning down officers on patrol.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Melissa Brannen: Missing : In 1989, residents of a close-knit apartment community in Virginia gathered for a holiday celebration. For Tammy Brannen and five-year-old daughter Melissa, Christmas was always a special time. Then without warning, the little girl was gone.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Fatal Friendship : In 1992, a woman disappeared from her Memphis home. When the ransom went uncollected, the FBI suspected a desperate amateur and feared the worst.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Ivy League Murder : Etna, New Hampshire is a small town. It's the kind of place where people trust their neighbors and leave their doors unlocked. A double murder shattered that trust and left the entire community terrified.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Independence Day Breakout : In 1987 the New Mexico Penitentiary housed hundreds of violent offenders, rapists, killers, men the juries put behind bars to protect society. On July fourth, seven of those men escaped.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Master Plan : Willow Springs, Illinois, seemed like any other quiet Midwestern community. But it was really one of the most corrupt places in the nation. This tiny village, nestled just outside of Chicago, was in the hip pocket of organized crime.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Operation Goldenrod : In the 1980s, the United States faced a deadly new enemy abroad, an expanding network of terrorists targeting American interests. Hundreds of innocent people were killed in bombings, executions, and hijackings.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Bad Company : In Boston, FBI agents and local police tracked a gang of robbers who specialized in armored car takedowns. Their MO was familiar. The gang used precise timing to steal millions in cash and burned their getaway cars to destroy evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Random Targets : Most victims of multiple murderers are meticulously chosen because of a mutual connection with the killer or because they match an intricate set of criteria that fits the killer's MO.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Fatal Abduction : Anytime, anywhere, people disappear, kidnapped from their daily routines. Predators always leave clues behind, but chasing them takes time, hampering investigators' attempts to solve these fatal abductions.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Web of Clues : Forensic Entomology: Bugs have roamed the earth for 250 million years, but their intimate association with death is just now coming to life.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Deadly Dealings : For a price, anything's possible. Contract killings arose out of the need to establish the perfect alibi. By hiring someone else to do the dirty work, a person can deflect guilt, at least in theory.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Traces Of Guilt : Sometimes the scales of justice are balanced by just a few milligrams of evidence  a couple of fibers, a tooth filling, or a smudge on a piece of tape. The biggest cases are often solved by the smallest clues.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Burning Evidence : Identifying Burned Remains: It's difficult to have a murder investigation without a body, and burning up the victim is a time-honored method of destroying physical evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Predators And Parasites : For the forensic entomologist, the insects that nest in dead bodies are like tiny witnesses to a crime.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Loved To Death : A victim usually never foresees danger when the perpetrator turns out to be a friend or a lover. Science and microscopic evidence can unmask these killers and find justice for those who are Loved To Death.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Material Witness : Lies and deceit can often throw investigators off the trail of justice. But when hard evidence contradicts a killer's story, police must use the clues to piece together the truth.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Family Plots : Every family has its secrets, and sometimes blood relations lead to bloodshed. When murder becomes a family affair, investigators must turn to forensics to uncover family plots.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: True Crime : They know as much about crime as any crime fighter, or any criminal. They're the crime writers, and through their eyes we see murder most foul.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Coroner Investigator : CI: Coroner Investigator will reveal the most in-depth look to date into the science of death.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Out To Kill : An abandoned car outside Philadelphia brings heartbreak to a family and terror to a community. A young woman is dead, the killer gone. But the marks of his passage remain.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Proof Of Innocence : DNA analysis overturns the convictions of three men who have spent years behind bars, paying for crimes they did not commit.

programme.title: Baywatch

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Here Comes Your Man, Pt. 1 : When Peter receives a new car for his birthday, he quickly becomes a frequent face in the street racing scene.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: True Colours : Emma is happy that the racing debacle is behind them, and that Peter is finally free if you don’t count the house arrest and five hundred hours of community service.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: What's It Feel Like to be a Ghost? Pt. 1 : Craig’s back in town for a music festival – and Manny can’t help picking up right where they left off.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: What's It Feel Like to be a Ghost? Pt. 2 : Ellie thinks all Craig’s bad behaviour is due to Manny – to help him, she’s determined to stay by his side while he’s in town.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Rock This Town : Manny’s intent on getting over Craig, and uses Liberty’s upcoming birthday as an excuse to throw a party at Emma’s house while Spike and Snake are out of town.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: The Bitterest Pill : In shock after over JT’s death, Toby turns to Liberty, convinced that his somber funeral isn’t what JT would’ve wanted. Liberty agrees, and the two grief-stricken friends plan to throw a celebratory memorial for JT at Degrassi.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: If You Leave : Manny’s parents want her to come home after the tragedy at Emma’s house – and to Emma’s surprise, Manny agrees.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Free Fallin', Pt. 1 : After a spectacular panic attack during a midterm exam, Paige’s professor at Banting offers her one more chance – which comes in the form of a ton of work to be completed over the holiday weekend.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Free Fallin', Pt. 2 : With her degree in jeopardy thanks to her failing grades, and the little issue of a dramatic dorm fire, Paige develops a Banting allergy. She slinks home to Toronto with her tail between her legs – but nobody can know the truth about what happened.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Love My Way : Paige has one goal: to return to her former pre-Banting glory. She’s going to get back to doing what she does best. Everything – and that includes dating. Paige surprises Spinner by asking him out. Nothing serious, just casual.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Sunglasses At Night : Marco’s at loose ends with Dylan gone and nothing seems exciting. He and Spinner decide a spring break road trip will break the monotony – but cash is the key.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Don't You Want Me, Pt. 1 : Alex and her mother Emily are in trouble - Chad, Emily's boyfriend, has run up thousands of dollars in credit card debt and hasn't been paying the rent.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Don't You Want Me, Pt. 2 : Alex's double life is finally bringing in the cash she wants but hiding everything from everyone is taking a toll on her schoolwork and her connection with Paige.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Standing In The Dark, Pt. 1 : The corridors of Degrassi have become a pressure cooker. With an unexpected influx of students from Lakehurst, Degrassi students find themselves facing old enemies and new friends.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Standing In The Dark, Pt. 2 : Abstinence-pledged Darcy wakes up in a strange bed, screamingly hung-over. Panic-stricken, she tries to piece together the events of the night before. She knows – physically – she had sex, but everything else is a blank.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Love is a Battlefield : Paige is ecstatic to have landed herself a gig in the fashion world. If everything goes well, this could turn itself into a brand new career path.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: It's Tricky : After seeing Craig’s newly released album on the shelves at the record store, Ashley decides to pursue her own music – starting with a performance at the Lakehurst-Degrassi talent mixer.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Death or Glory, Pt. 1 : Amidst the crowded classrooms and the battle for school territory, tensions between Lakehurst and Degrassi students continue to mount. But Spinner’s got his eye on enemy territory -- the adorably tough new Lakehurst girl, Jane.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Death or Glory, Pt. 2 : Faced with his own aggression and looming mortality, Spinner attempts to prove his ‘masculinity’ by kicking an unnecessary amount of Lakehurst ass.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: We Got The Beat : Manny’s moved back home but the honeymoon between she and her parents is just about over. And when her dad insists she have a traditional “Debut” – the lavish party many Filipino girls have for their 18th birthday – Manny takes a stand.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Jessie's Girl : Ellie’s ex-mentor Caitlin Ryan arrives to do a guest lecture series in Jesse’s class and Ellie sees her opportunity to set herself apart from her peers. She throws herself into Caitlin’s class assignment and her hard work quickly pays off.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Hungry Eyes : Emma decides she’ll do what it takes to get back on track, starting by rekindling her friendship with Manny, but when Manny wants to hit an audition to be a ‘Purple Dragon’ sports drink model, Emma finds herself quickly at odds with her best friend.

programme.title: Degrassi: The Next Generation

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's News! : Blue has big news and we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Steve Gets the Sniffles : Steve has the sniffles. We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue thinks Steve needs to feel better.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Does Blue Want To Build? : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to build.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's Senses : Blue leaves tactile, visual, and audio clues for us and we play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to play next.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Experiment Does Blue Want To Try? : Blue and Steve are experimenting by playing sink and float, but Blue wants to try another experiment. We play Blue's Clues to figure out which experiment Blue wants to try next.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Does Blue Want To Make Out of Recycled Things? : Blue and Steve are busy cleaning. Steve throws things away but Blue wants to use old things to make something new. We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to make.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Was Blue's Dream About? : Blue had a dream and we play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue's dream was about.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's ABCs : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what book Blue wants to read.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Math! : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to buy. Along the way we help Mr. Salt and Mrs. Pepper figure out who has more apples, how to make them equal, and the total number they have all together.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's Birthday : Everyone's invited to Blue's big event! We play Blue's Clues to figure out what present Blue wants for her birthday.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Does Blue Want To Do With Her Picture? : Blue drew a picture and we play Blue's Clues to figure what Blue wants to do with it.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Does Blue Wanna Do on a Rainy Day? : As it starts to rain, Steve and Blue discover that the raindrops sound like music. Blue decides she would like to do something musical, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to do on this rainy day.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's Surprise at Two O'Clock : Blue has a surprise for us, but it won't be ready until 2 o'clock. Using our watch, we time activities and discover the difference between seconds and minutes while we wait for our surprise.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: The Lost Episode! : We play Blue's Clues to figure out where Blue was when she lost her backpack. Guided by a hand saying -- Go back, go back, go back to where you were!

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue's Sad Day : Something is making Blue sad, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is. Along the way, we talk about feelings and figure out how to resolve difficult situations and emotions.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Game Does Blue Want to Learn? : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what game Blue wants to learn. In the meantime we solve number problems, practice learning our lefts from our rights, and jump into the felt board to play tic-tac-toe with O-Friend.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Did Blue See? : It's Blue's Clues from a whole new perspective! We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue saw. Along the way, we see things through mirrors and kaleidoscopes, from up above and down below.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Nurture! : We help Steve and Blue take care of their plants, and now there's something else that we need to do. We play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Blue is Frustrated : Something is frustrating Blue, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is. We learn to "stop, take a deep breath, and think" when we find ourselves in frustrating situations.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What Is Blue Trying To Do? : Blue wants to show us something she's been trying to do, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Mechanics! : We help Steve and Blue fix their broken toy plane. There's something else to fix, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Art Appreciation : We are having a big art show and we play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue is making for the show.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Weight and Balance : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what Blue wants to do. Throughout the day, with the help of our scale friends, we compare the weight of objects and determine which are heavy and which are light.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Geography : We play Blue's Clues to figure out where Blue wants to go. Using a map, we help Shovel and Pail figure out which buildings would be best suited for specific errands then we decide what form of transportation would work best.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: What's that Sound? : Blue hears something that we can't hear, so we play Blue's Clues to figure out what it is. Led by our ears, we help Shovel and Pail follow the path a gopher took in the woods, based on the sounds he makes.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Signs : Guided by signs and symbols around the house, we play Blue's Clues to figure out where Blue wants to have her snack.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Hide and Seek : Steve and Blue are playing Hide and Seek and it's Blue's turn to hide. We play Blue's Clues to figure out where Blue wants to hide.

programme.title: Blue's Clues
programme.desc: Animal Behavior : We play Blue's Clues to figure out what animal Blue wants to learn about. Throughout the episode, we learn about different animal behaviors by observing them and pretending to be them.

programme.desc: Timmy 2000 : Lors de l'interro de début de cours, Timmy, un nouvel élève handicapé ne sait pas répondre. Il est alors envoyé chez la principale. Les médecins lui diagnostiquent un déficit aigu de l'attention, qui le prive de devoirs.

programme.title: South Park: Pip!
programme.desc: Pip! : Cet épisode spécial est consacré à Pip, personnage très spécial inspiré d'un roman de Charles Dickens intitulé 'Les Grandes Espérances'. Pip est un jeune forgeron et va devoir trouver une solution pour conquérir le cœur de la belle Estella.

programme.title: South Park: Cartman adhère à la Nambla
programme.desc: Cartman adhère à la Nambla : Cartman en a marre des autres. Pour lui ils ne sont pas assez matures. Il décide de trouver des amis adultes sur Internet, mais tous ses amis se font envoyer en prison. Il va voir Mephesto pour avoir un ami artificiel.

programme.title: South Park: Tampons en Cheveux de Cherok
programme.desc: Tampons en Cheveux de Cherok : Kyle tombe gravement malade, et en ville tout le monde ne jure que par la médecine à base de plantes des indiens. La mère de Kyle refuse alors de faire hospitaliser son fils croyant qu'il ira mieux avec les plantes.

programme.title: South Park: Chef Pète les Plombs
programme.desc: Chef Pète les Plombs : Chef est scandalisé : le drapeau de South Park est selon lui raciste. Afin d'en juger avec la plus grande impartialité, il est demandé aux enfants de décider s'il faut changer ou non le drapeau.

programme.title: South Park: Un Truc Qu'on Peut Faire Avec le Doigt
programme.desc: Un Truc Qu'on Peut Faire Avec le Doigt : Cartman décide de mettre à exécution le rêve qu'il vient de faire : monter un groupe de musique avec les autres garçons pour gagner '10 millions de dollars'.

programme.title: South Park: Les handicapés vont-ils en enfer?
programme.desc: Les handicapés vont-ils en enfer? : Le Pasteur décide de parler de l'enfer. Il fait alors peur aux enfants en leur disant que s'ils ne sont pas de bons chrétiens, ils périront dans les flammes de l'enfer. Les enfants, décident alors de devenir de bons chrétiens.

programme.title: South Park: Probablement
programme.desc: Probablement : Dans l'épisode précédent, les garçons découvrent le Pasteur en plein acte sexuel dans son église. Cartman décide de prendre les choses en main afin de remettre dans le droit chemin tous les enfants de la ville.

programme.title: South Park: CM1
programme.desc: CM1 : C'est la rentrée. Les garçons arrivent en CM1, heureux de ne plus se faire taper par les grands du CM1. Mais ce sont désormais les CM2 qui les tapent. La nouvelle prof Mme Chrokelpaf, ainsi que la nouvelle façon de travailler les effrayent.

programme.title: South Park: Le Super Classeur
programme.desc: Le Super Classeur : Cartman vient d'acheter un tout nouveau classeur avec toutes les fonctionnalités. C'est alors qu'un homme venu du futur le prévient que ce classeur peut envahir la planète. Le classeur est donc détruit, mais Cartman s'en rachète un le lendemain.

programme.title: South Park: Helen Keller! The Musical
programme.desc: Helen Keller! The Musical : Thanksgiving approche et comme tous les ans, les CM1 doivent jouer la vie d'Helene Keller. Butters s'est infiltré chez les maternelles et leur annonce que leur spectacle est superbe.

programme.title: South Park: Camp de Régime
programme.desc: Camp de Régime : Cartman est envoyé en Camp de Régime car tous ses proches trouvent que c'est mauvais pour lui d'être gros. Il décide alors de tirer profit de cette expérience, mais pas de la manière dont ses proches le souhaiteraient.

programme.title: South Park: Le Mot en M
programme.desc: Le Mot en M : Les enfants veulent aller voir un concert, mais les parents de Kyle refusent de le laisser y aller. Kyle décide alors de trouver un moyen pour se débarrasser d'eux. Cartman lui conseille d'appeler la police et de dire qu'il se fait maltraiter.

programme.title: South Park: Le Cycle du Caca
programme.desc: Le Cycle du Caca : Cette année, les garçons ont peur de ne pas avoir de cadeaux de Noël. Les gens n'achètent plus. Kyle décide alors d'aller voir Mr Hankey pour l'aider a redonner l'esprit de Noël.

programme.title: South Park: Scott Tenorman doit mourir
programme.desc: Scott Tenorman doit mourir : Cartman voudrait être le premier de la bande à atteindre la puberté. Il achète donc des 'poils' 10 dollars à un garçon plus âgé, Scott Tenorman.

programme.title: South Park: Y'en a Dans Le Ventilo
programme.desc: Y'en a Dans Le Ventilo : La série Cop Drama va diffuser un épisode ou le mot 'Merde' sera prononcé. Dès lors une épidémie de peste se déclare.

programme.title: South Park: Combat d'Infirme
programme.desc: Combat d'Infirme : Al Super Gay est viré des scouts parce qu'il est gay. Et les enfants font la connaissance de Jimmy Valmer, un handicapé qui prend la vie avec humour. Mais Timmy est jaloux du succès de Jimmy, et un combat d'infirmes est engagé.

programme.title: South Park: Terrance et Phillip : de tempêtes en naufrages
programme.desc: Terrance et Phillip : de tempêtes en naufrages : Terrence et Phillip, les canadiens pétomanes, mettent fin à leur duo pour divergences créatives. Les garçons se mettent en tête de réunir leurs idoles.

programme.title: South Park: Cartman Land
programme.desc: Cartman Land : Suite à la mort de sa grand-mère, Cartman hérite d'un million de dollars et réalise son rêve de toujours: il achète un parc d'attraction (dont il profite tout seul) qu'il rebaptise 'Cartmanland '.

programme.title: South Park: Du bon usage du preservatif
programme.desc: Du bon usage du preservatif : Les parents découvrent que leurs enfants sont très mal informés sur les choses du sexe. On demande à l'école de donner des cours. À croire que les parents ignorent qui sont les enseignants, Mme Crockelpaf, Mr Mackey et Mr Garrison.

programme.title: South Park: Servietsky
programme.desc: Servietsky : À peine les enfants ont-ils obtenu leur console Gamesphère qu'on leur enlève. Ils doivent livrer une serviette intelligente fumant des pétards a une organisation militaire. Seulement les gamins se retrouvent embrouillés dans une histoire.

programme.title: South Park: Oussama Ben Laden pue du cul
programme.desc: Oussama Ben Laden pue du cul : Kenny, Cartman, Stan et Kyle sont envoyés par erreur en Afghanistan. Sur place et contre toute attente, ils vont s'apercevoir qu'ils ont beaucoup de points communs avec les enfants Afghans.

programme.title: South Park: Comment manger avec son cul
programme.desc: Comment manger avec son cul : Cartman placarde la photo de Kenny associée à la mention 'Enfant disparu' sur tous les packs de lait du pays.

programme.title: South Park: L'Engin
programme.desc: L'Engin : Mr Garrison, ne supportant plus que ses vols d'avions soient annulés ou retardés, décide d'inventer un nouveau moyen de transport.Kyle est très embarrassé quand il reçoit la visite de son cousin de New-York, également prénommé Kyle.

programme.title: South Park: Les riches debarquent
programme.desc: Les riches debarquent : Se sentant rejeté, Token décide d'appeler d'autres riches. Mais les hommes de la ville ne supportent pas l'arrivée de ces riches.

programme.title: South Park: Kenny Se Meurt
programme.desc: Kenny Se Meurt : Cartman tente de sauver la vie de Kenny en suppliant le Congrès de faire reprendre la recherche sur les cellules souches.

programme.title: South Park: L'Episode de Butters
programme.desc: L'Episode de Butters : Butters fait quelques découvertes qui vont changer le cours de son existence. Son père est bisexuel et sa mère tente de le tuer. Heureusement tout s'arrange à la fin.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: Couillo-mentonite : Le Maury Povich Show reçoit des personnes difformes et leur offre des cadeaux. Les gamins décident d'envoyer Butters au show avec des testicules en latex attachées au menton.

programme.desc: Bobby's Hero : Bobby becomes obsessed with Jesse James. Mike and Carol don't approve of his new hero, and try to find a way to convince him that James was nothing but a "mean, dirty killer."

programme.title: Leave It To Bryan
programme.desc: As Bad as it Gets : Tony and Simone just want a functional home for their young family. After Bryan makes his choice, this couple can’t believe what their home was hiding.

programme.title: Leave It To Bryan
programme.desc: Path of Most Resistance : Jody looks to hire a professional before her husband realizes he just can't take on the renovations himself.

programme.title: Leave It To Bryan

programme.title: Leave It To Bryan
programme.desc: Bricking Time Bomb : Kelsey and Chris are taking on a lofty reno right before their wedding. Bryan is used to planning renovations, not disrupting wedding plans, but he might not have a choice this time.

programme.title: Leave It To Bryan

programme.desc: Quite a Spectacle : It was one of the most unusual cases in forensic history. Investigators had to find a way to solve a murder case using only a squashed tomato found at the crime scene and tiny, pinpoint reflections of light in a photograph.

programme.desc: Innocence Lost : Sometime during a neighborhood Christmas party, five-year-old Melissa Brannen disappeared. Investigators turned to forensic science to help them see what the witnesses missed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Without a Prayer : In 1995, the founder of American Atheists disappeared with one of her sons, her granddaughter and $500,000 in gold coins. The police investigated several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Transaction Failed : When a dedicated, well-respected teacher disappeared, police had to determine if she'd gone on vacation without telling anyone or if she was the victim of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Cries Unheard : A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car; the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Unholy Alliance : When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes. A pair of bloody gloves, unique tire tracks and ordinary pine needles provided investigators with some extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Broken Bond : Doctors are puzzled when a two-year-old boy is rushed to the hospital with brain seizures and breathing difficulties. Finally, the boy's sister provides an important clue, raising the possibility of a rare syndrome.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Jean Pool : In 1984, the body of college student Laura Salmon was found on a Middle Tennessee farm, covered with her own denim jeans as well as the jeans of the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: True Lies : In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months and years passed, but the crime was never solved. Finally, a private investigator discovered Kathleen's diary.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Smiley Face : The woman had been sexually assaulted, stabbed repeatedly and left for dead. When a perfect suspect practically turned himself in, police were sure they had their man — until DNA evidence proved them wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hear No Evil : In 2006, Darlene VanderGiesen received threatening emails and then disappeared. Tracking the source of the emails led police to the home of Daphne Wright, where they believe a murder was committed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Hair Line : Doctors don't know why a young scientist has fallen gravely ill. When tests finally reveal the cause, it's too late to save him.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Disrobed : The crime scene was especially violent: a husband and wife had been shot to death in their bedroom. Was their 16-year-old daughter complicit?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: One for the Road : A married couple decided to escape the cold of winter with a mini-vacation in Key West. The wife went missing and police searched every square inch of the island; they found nothing but a pair of sandals which might have belonged to her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In Harm's Way : An Alaskan police officer finds a woman's body while on routine patrol of a public park. During the autopsy, the medical examiner preserves the portion of her rib cage which sustained the fatal wound.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Constructive Criticism : In 2000, construction manager Darrell North was found dead in his work trailer in Ft. Worth, Texas. When investigators arrived, they found that he had been stabbed 46 times, indicating a personal relationship between the victim and the attacker.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Step by Step : When a wealthy socialite died after falling down the stairs, witness accounts contradicted evidence. To find out what really happened, investigators employed a physicist and an expert in accident reconstruction.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Catch 22 : In 1996, 54-year-old Gayle Isleib was ambushed in her Manchester, Connecticut driveway and shot to death. Police had to determine whether obsessive love was the motive for her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Partners in Crime : In a 1992 murder case, the autopsy of a cremated body turns up telltale clues of foul play.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Scout's Honor : In 1984, a passerby finds the torso of a woman in a cardboard box. A forensic entomologist analyzes nearby insect activity to determine when the victim was killed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Killer's Cattle Log : When police in the Great Plains are called about a dead body, a background check on the victim leads them into a bizarre web of homeless drifters, cattle auctions and bad checks — all fronted by elderly couple Ray and Faye Copeland.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Woman Scorned : When Joann Katrinak is found dead in a corn field with her infant son, the obvious suspect is the husband and father of the victims. But insects found on the bodies reveal a vital clue which uncovers a twisted story of passion gone wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Sign of the Zodiac : For six years, a serial killer prowled the streets of New York City. He wrote letters to police and The New York Post, indicating he would kill twelve people — one for each astrological sign.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Point of Origin : In 1984, California firefighters battled ten arson fires in three weeks. When cigarettes and a scrap of paper connected the case to similar fires further north, the hunt was on for a dangerous pyromaniac.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Micro-Clues : After a day of fishing in a quiet village in Switzerland, a teenage boy doesn't return home as planned. The investigation reveals some important microscopic evidence in the water near where he was last seen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Sole Survivor : A Michigan State University grad student disappeared and was presumed dead. With the help of a professor of geological sciences, police hoped to get the "dirt" on her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Bitter Potion : Flu-like symptoms of a mother and her children prove to be something more serious: thallium poisoning. After the mother's death, investigators turn their attention to finding the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Room with a View : A young woman is stabbed more than 100 times. The killer leaves DNA behind, but investigators must play a cat-and-mouse game to obtain a suspect's DNA to match.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Colonel's Wife : Ret. Col. George Maracek, a 69-year-old former Green Beret who served in Korea and Vietnam, has been charged with the 1991 murder of his Thai wife Viparet. He maintains that he is innocent.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Murder at Sea? : The disappearance of newlywed George Smith, who vanished from a cruise in the Mediterranean. This report uncovers the questionable alibis, failed polygraphs and a provocative video that involves the three men who last saw Smith alive.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Imposter : A report about an elusive con man David Pecard, who has taken on more than 20 identities throughout his life of crime. Pecard has infiltrated police departments and top military posts, put criminals behind bars and even posed as a technician.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Murder by Design : Investigators question whether designer Gianni Versace was the random victim of a killing spree, or he knew the man who shot him to death.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Hollywood Secrets : The investigation into the 2008 murder of Juliana Redding, an aspiring actress and model and the subsequent trial. Redding was found dead in her Santa Monica, California home

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Last Chance for Justice : A report on the 1980 murder of Marilyn McIntyre, who was found dead in her home. Her husband, Lane, had returned home from work that morning and found her body. As police checked into his alibi, he named his friend Curt Forbes as a potential suspect.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Deadly Prophecy : Faylene Eaves, divorced Arizona mother of two, married Doug Grant, a divorced single dad, in 1993. They were both Mormons. Faylene had revelations from God that could have contributed to her death, however her husband was found guilty of murdering her.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: The Last Confession : Relatives of a beauty queen who was murdered more than 50 years ago suspect a priest committed the crime.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Driven to Extremes : Authorities investigate a case of love, infidelity, road rage and possible murder in Texas.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Prime Suspect : Billy Fox, 33, is a devoted father of two and a soccer coach. He appears to be an all-around regular guy. But he’s booked for the death of a baby. It's a charge that could put him in prison for the rest of his life.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Temptation : In June 2007, Dewayne Barrentine met Tausha Fields and soon after they began living together. Tausha told him some unbelievable stories about herself – leading Barrentine to verify her stories online, which resulted in the uncovering of a homicide.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Death Without Mercy : On November 29, 2005, firemen put out a fire in a house in Morgantown, West Virginia -- and discovered the charred remains of Jimmy Michael and his wife Shelly. A medical examiner’s report found that Jimmy was dead before the blaze had even started.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Deadly Proposal : Stephanie Pepper Sims was a college professor in the prime of her life when she suddenly vanished without a trace. Estranged from her husband, Stephanie moved in with a lover. Eventually, a hand-drawn map would lead investigators her body.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Chasing the Barefoot Bandit : A report on Colton Harris-Moore, also known as the “Barefoot Bandit,” because of his habit of committing crimes barefoot and leaving chalk outlines of his feet; and whose exploits included stealing planes, guns, yachts, and escaping to foreign countries.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Guilty Until Proven Innocent : Two men convicted for a brutal rape they say they didn’t commit, and DNA evidence backs them up. How did a law professor, her students and new DNA technology play a part in the case?

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Reasonable Doubt : In September 1999, Brian Eftenoff of Phoenix, Az. Was convicted of murdering his wife, Judi. It was alleged that Eftenoff forced more than a gram of cocaine down his wife’s throat causing her to die of a cocaine-induced stroke.

programme.title: 48 Hours
programme.desc: Resident Evil : The investigation into two double murders committed five years apart leads police to a famous hospital and an unlikely suspect.

programme.title: Paranormal State

programme.title: Paranormal State
programme.desc: Haunted Sex Dungeon : Ken, the owner of a sprawling mansion on Greyhaven Island near Detroit, says he feels more like the caretaker. Voices, banging and a run of bad luck have him feeling both watched and cursed.

programme.title: Paranormal State

programme.title: Paranormal State

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Ghosts Of War : When Dennis Pongratz finds his deceased father's Army belongings, he unlocks a treasure trove of WWII memorabilia, including an armband removed from a Nazi soldier killed in battle. Soon the Pongratz home becomes a battleground between good and evil.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Vision Of Terror : When Stacy Stowe hears ghostly sounds in her home, she assumes it's the spirit of her late husband who died in a tragic car accident. But a psychic warns Stacy that she will find no peace until she closes the portal that has opened the door for evil.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Fear Feeders : Soon after Al Gonzalez purchases a home outside Fort Wayne, Indiana, he begins to hear strange noises and sees unexplainable shadows. Paranormal investigators determine the entities in Al's home are dangerous and that he is not their first victim.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Ghost Confessions : An unknown entity appears to find pleasure in terrifying 17-year-old Macy Sherman, her younger sister Kelsey and their mom Carrie. The family reaches out for help, praying that they can get rid of the spirit intent on staying in their home forever.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Love Curse : When Lee and his new bride Claire settle into their first home, an evil entity emerges and attacks Claire while she's carrying the couple's unborn baby. A ghost hunter determines the dark spirit is a demon and brings in demonologists to cleanse the home.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Demon's Lair : For years, the Cranmer family thought their historic Pittsburgh dream home was haunted by a harmless ghost. But, when the activity grows violent, they fight back with the only weapon they have, prayer; culminating with an exorcism of the house.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Ghost Protector : A mother and daughter both believe their house is haunted. But while the mother believes it's the spirit of her murdered boyfriend, her daughter believes it's much darker. Paranormal experts investigate and uncover an epic battle between good and evil.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Immortal Love : Shortly after Whitney's family moving into their first home, a restless spirit shatters the serenity of their suburban life. Whitney calls in a paranormal team who uncover the truth behind the spirit's tragic death and his intense obsession with Whitney.

programme.title: A Haunting
programme.desc: Living Nightmare : Angela, a newlywed, is excited to start her new life with Stephen when nightmares that she experienced as a child suddenly resurface. Soon the menacing old hag from her dreams terrorizes her daughter and takes temporary possession of her son.

programme.title: A Haunting

Meanwhile, Bartolomeo pushes himself to the limit in order to help Luffy advance, but does he leave himself vulnerable in the process?

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: A Tearful Reunion! Rebecca and Kyros! : Diamante has Rebecca pinned and completely overpowered. If only the Soldier-san of her youth were able to protect her! Meanwhile, Luffy and Law finally surmount incredible odds to land themselves in striking distance of Doflamingo.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: One Shot One Kill! The Man Who Will Save Dressrosa! : Usopp uses Viola's Clairvoyance to prepare his ammunition to take Sugar out! But the slightest hesitation now will spell disaster, and the end of Luffy! Luckily for the Straw Hats, Usopp's ammunition has been specially designed for this situation!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: Anger Erupts! Luffy and Law's Ultimate Stratagem! : The final showdown with Doflamingo begins, but the Warlord still has several tricks up his sleeve - including turning one of Luffy's own friends against him! Meanwhile Franky and Señor Pink are in a standoff to find out who is the coolest.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: A Noble Family! The True Identity of Doflamingo! : All hopes rest on Luffy and Law being able to take down Doflamingo; but the Warlord has other plans in mind as he gives the two the fight of their lives, and reveals his true, shocking history! Meanwhile, Sabo finds out Fujitora's true intention…

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Ultimate Power! The Secret of the Op-Op Fruit! : With Fujitora's stalemate arousing suspicion in the Navy ranks, Law and Luffy stand seconds away from execution at the hand of Doflamingo! But before he dispatches them both, he shares the secret of his sway over the Celestial Dragons!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: Sad Memories! Law, the Boy from the White Town! : The horrible secret of Flevance, the White Town, is revealed, giving clues to Law's traumatic past! A rage born from the lies of the World Government created a child who had the will to kill Corazon!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: A Celestial Dragon! Doffy's Stormy Past! : Law's attack on Corazon looks less successful than he thought! And after he's accepted into the Doflamingo Family, Corazon's mysterious silence hides a fact that may save Law's life - and a hidden danger Law must face in Doflamingo himself!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: A Rocky Road! Law and Corazon's Journey of Life! : Things grow stranger for Law as he learns the secret behind why Corazon feigns being mute! But the mysterious Corazon's actions are only beginning to baffle Law as he takes him on a journey to cure his White Lead Disease.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Time is Ticking Down! Seize the Op-Op Fruit! : Corazon's double-dealing with the Navy raises suspicions both with Doflamingo and Law. But all that is secondary as Corazon finally has a chance to grab the Op-Op Fruit during a staged handoff arranged by the Government.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Moment of Resolution! Corazon's Farewell Smile! : The Op-Op Fruit is in Corazon's hands! But the price he paid to get it might be too high... When Law goes for help, unfortunate coincidences intervene and leave the two vulnerable! Meanwhile, Doflamingo and his clan finally land at Minion Island.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: Advance, Law! The Kindhearted Man's Final Fight! : Doflamingo & his Family begin to zero in on Corazon and Law's whereabouts, but Corazon has a plan to hide Law long enough to let him escape, even if that means sacrificing his own life. A chain of lucky coincidences allows the plan to be set into motion.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: To Be Free! Law's Injection Shot Blasts! : Violet runs down the status of the open battles raging in Dressrosa - the Straw Hats, the Tontattas, the executives of the Doflamingo Family - all of them clash around the island, but it's Law's revelation to Doflamingo that moves the action forward!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: An Intense Battle! Law vs. Doflamingo! : Luffy continues to battle Fake-mingo, but keeps accidentally battling Bellamy instead. Meanwhile the tide of battle turns against Law as painful scars from the past resurface. And Zoro gets unexpected help in his battle with Pica.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: A Decisive Battle Against the Executives! Proud Hajrudin! : Bellamy escapes Doflamingo's control, but it might be too late. His long history with Doflamingo unfolds and leads to an inglorious end. Meanwhile, Hajrudin has been beaten down, but a fire still burns in him to pay back the favor he owes!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Battle of Love! The New Leader Sai vs. Baby 5! : Lao G and Don Chinjao duke it out as each brings their most devastating attacks to the battle. But a misunderstanding caused by Baby 5 has Sai and Don Chinjao distracted - are they distracted enough for Lao G to gain the upper hand?

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Man's Pride! Bellamy's Last Charge! : Law is at the whim of Doflamingo and Trebol, while Luffy still has to deal with Bellamy, who fights to keep his honor even after being released by Doflamingo! Meanwhile, Gladius is about to detonate the ground around Cavendish and Bartolomeo!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: A Strong Wind and a Surge! Hakuba vs. Dellinger! : A mysterious force that resembles Cavendish menaces the battlefield - but while it appears to help Bartolomeo and the others at first, it sets its sights on Robin! Meanwhile, the deeply-wounded Ideo sees Dellinger's horrible true nature!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: Barrier-Barrier! Homage Holy Fist Strikes! : Bartolomeo is in a neck-and-neck battle against Gladius to save Robin, and is faced with an impossible dilemma. With all his options leading to their defeat, can his cunning ploy save the day? Meanwhile, Robin is face to face with Cavendish.

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Healing Princess! Save Mansherry! : Princess Mansherry's amazing power - to heal living beings completely - is in the hands of the Donquixote executive. If she is able to heal all the executives who've gone down in battle - the Straw Hats and their allies stand no chance!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: The Manly Duel! Señor's Elegy of Love! : Señor Pink and Franky continue their knock-down, drag-out, manly fight. As both of them are pushed to their limits, we learn Señor's tragic past! Meanwhile, Princess Mansherry's rescue hits a snag as she stubbornly refuses to leave!

programme.title: One Piece
programme.desc: Stardust of Death! Diamante's Storm of Vicious Attacks! : Franky and Señor Pink end their harrowing duel in a manly way - but victory knocks the wind out of even the winner's sails! Meanwhile, Diamante and Kyros struggle desperately on the Sunflower Field, each demonstrating their extreme prowess.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's Rescue in Mermaid Kingdom : Dora and Boots are at the beach when they find a little lost mermaid who needs to get back to her mommy at Mermaid Cove. Together the 3 set off on an underwater adventure!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: ¡Vamos a Pintar! : Dora and Boots discover Pincel, a cute little paintbrush who's lost and really wants to get back to Painter Daniella at the Art Studio.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora Rocks! : Today's Benny's Sing-Along Party where Dora and Benny are going to sing a very special song together! C'mon, let's all sing-along!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: The Butterfly Ball : Dora and Boots take a rainbow butterfly, Mariposa, to the Butterfly Ball where everybody gets to have wings.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Diego's Amazing Animal Circus Adventure : Dora and Diego's Amazing Animal Circus Adventure: Join Dora and Diego on an adventure to see Boots and Baby Jaguar's Amazing Animal Circus, featuring Perrito, the kitties, Tico, Isa, Benny, and lots more animal friends!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Diego's Amazing Animal Circus Adventure : Dora and her cousin Diego are on their way to a big surprise. It's Boots and Baby Jaguar's Amazing Animal Circus!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Book Explorers : Dora and Boots become book explorers and enter the worlds of Peter Pan, the Wizard of Oz, and Little Red Riding Hood so they can find missing objects for the library's Story Wall.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's Super Soccer Showdown : Dora's Super Soccer Showdown: Dora travels to Brazil to play in the Big Cup Soccer Tournament, and if her team can beat the Monsters, they'll win the big cup.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora and Diego in the Time of Dinosaurs : Dora and Diego in the Time of Dinosaurs: Dora and Diego travel back to prehistoric times and go on a dinosaur adventure to find Baby Jaguar and bring him home to his birthday party.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora in Wonderland : Dora in Wonderland: While Dora's kitties run through a magic mirror, Dora becomes Dora in Wonderland! Join her and Boots on a double-length adventure through the magical, mixed-up world of Wonderland!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora's Royal Rescue : Dora's Royal Rescue: Dora and Boots and the noble horse, Rocinante, are off to Story Castle to rescue Don Quixote from the evil wizard, Malambruno.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Lost and Found : Lost and Found: Dora and Boots find a little baby blue bird who fell out of her nest.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Hic-Boom-Ohhh : Hic-Boom-Ohhh: What's that funny sound coming from Yellow Valley? Dora and Boots set off to investigate. They'll have to go across the Noisy River, then tiptoe through the Quiet Forest, and that's how they'll get to Yellow Valley to find the sound.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Ice Cream : Ice Cream: Deedle dee dee! Dora and Boots hear the ice cream truck nearby, but they just miss it.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Choo Choo : Choo Choo: All aboard! It's time for the big train race. First train to the Big Yellow Station wins a big whistle.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Treasure Island : Treasure Island: Dora and Boots find a blue key; it turns out it's the key to a big treasure chest.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Three L'il Piggies : Three L'il Piggies: Dora and Boots are at the farm today, bringing presents to the three little piggies.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Big River : Big River: Boots loses one of his shiny red boots in the Big River. Dora and Boots set off on a wild water adventure as they chase the runaway boot.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Berry Hunt : Berry Hunt: Dora and Boots go hunting for blueberries on Blueberry Hill. But they'll have to be careful--Swiper the fox lives on Blueberry Hill!

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: WizzleWishes : WizzleWishes: Dora and Boots meet Wizzle who lost his bag of wishes and can't get home. Our heroes, Dora and Boots, set off in search of the lost wishes.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Dora the Explorer : Grandma's House: Dora and Boots set off to deliver a basket of treats to Dora's Abuela to help her cold. Dora's mother warns her to watch out for Swiper the Fox. He'll swipe the treats.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Surprise : Surprise: It's Boots' birthday! Dora tells him that all his friends are waiting at her house to give Boots a big party.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Sticky Tape : Sticky Tape: Benny the Bull needs some Sticky Tape in a hurry. His hot air balloon is leaking, and floating toward Crocodile Lake! Dora and Boots rush off to rescue their friend.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Bouncing Ball : Bouncing Ball: Boots buys a super blue bouncy ball at the store, but it bounces off down the hill.

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Backpack : Backpack: Dora remembers the first time she met her friend Backpack. Backpack is helping Dora carry her books back to the Library. Can they reach the Library before it closes?

programme.title: Dora the Explorer
programme.desc: Bugga Bugga : Bugga Bugga: Dora and Boots offer to help Mama Bugga Bugga bring a giant cookie home to feed her babies.

programme.title: When Sparks Fly

programme.title: All of My Heart
programme.desc: All of My Heart : A young caterer’s life suddenly changes course when she inherits a country home and learns she must share it with a career-obsessed Wall Street trader. At first, these opposites do not attract...

programme.title: Valentines Match
programme.desc: Valentines Match : Fired from her job as a reality TV host, Natalie returns home for Valentine's Day, only to find herself running the town festival's auction with her ex-fiancé thanks to two scheming mothers.

programme.title: My Best Friends Bouquet
programme.desc: My Best Friends Bouquet : Josie Hughes is a hopeless romantic who believes in the kind of romance in black-and-white movies where the leading lady finds her one true love and gets swept off her feet...

programme.title: Our Italian Christmas Memories
programme.desc: Our Italian Christmas Memories : The Colucci siblings, in an effort to jog the memory of their grandfather who is struggling with dementia, set out to recreate their late grandmother’s legendary pasta sauce.

programme.title: Love in Winterland
programme.desc: Love in Winterland : Ally, a final contestant on a dating show, must face her high school sweetheart when she is chosen for the Hometown Date.

programme.title: Ghosts of Christmas Past
programme.desc: Ghosts of Christmas Past : Ellie is told by a fortune teller that she must resolve her past and make amends with all of those she ghosted before Christmas and discovers love in the process.

programme.title: A Royal Corgi Christmas
programme.desc: A Royal Corgi Christmas : Prince Edmond hopes to win his mother's love back with the gift of an adorable but unruly corgi. He hires American dog trainer Cecily to make the corgi presentable for the annual Christmas ball.

programme.title: Royal Nanny
programme.desc: Royal Nanny : MI5 agent Claire goes undercover as the royal nanny, and must overcome the challenges of her assignment, like resisting the charms of Prince Colin while keeping the family safe at Christmas.

programme.title: Sense, Sensibility and Snowmen
programme.desc: Sense, Sensibility and Snowmen : Ella and her sister Marianne are HUGE Christmas enthusiasts and own a party planning business. One of their new clients is a toy company and they don't see eye-to-eye with Edward, the company's CEO.

programme.title: Finding Father Christmas
programme.desc: Finding Father Christmas : Christmas for Miranda is a painful reminder of the family she never had. Her mother died when she was young, never revealing the identity of her father. But, three days before Christmas, she unexpectedly receives a clue.

programme.title: Must Love Christmas
programme.desc: Must Love Christmas : Known for her Christmas-themed stories, a renowned romance novelist finds herself snowbound in the charming town of Cranberry Falls. She soon finds herself in an unexpected love triangle with her childhood crush and a determined reporter.

programme.title: Christmas at Dollywood
programme.desc: Christmas at Dollywood : When a NYC event planner returns to Tennessee to plan the Christmas celebration at Dollywood, she's paired with the head of operations who thinks he can handle the party just fine on his own.

programme.title: Dying To Be You
programme.desc: Dying To Be You : Miles apart, Molly and Lily become pen pals at 4th grade, continuing to write to each other throughout their lives. Things change however when Molly shows up at Lily's 25th Birthday...

programme.title: My Mothers Killer Boyfriend
programme.desc: My Mothers Killer Boyfriend : When sweet and impressionable single mother Nicole is set up with Jonathan, a charismatic and handsome professor, she's immediately swept off her feet and is willing to give love a chance again.

programme.title: Obsession Stalked by My Lover
programme.desc: Obsession Stalked by My Lover : A woman's romantic relationship with her new roommate turns treacherous when he hatches a scheme to steal money from her wealthy sister.

programme.title: Witness to Murder A Darrow Mystery
programme.desc: Witness to Murder A Darrow Mystery : Claire Darrow defends a woman accused of murder, who used to be a friend of her mother, Joanna Darrow.

programme.title: She Went Missing
programme.desc: She Went Missing : A reporter's long-dormant stalker reemerges when she returns to her hometown to investigate the disappearance of her childhood best friend.

programme.title: Ann Rules A House on Fire
programme.desc: Ann Rules A House on Fire : The true story of Debora Queen, who was sentenced to thirty years in prison for the fire that burned down her house and caused the death of her son in 1995.

programme.title: Abducted The Mary Stauffer Story
programme.desc: Abducted The Mary Stauffer Story : High school teacher Mary Stauffer and her eight-year old daughter, Beth are held captive for 53 days by an obsessed former student. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Tempted By Danger
programme.desc: Tempted By Danger : An attorney starts dating an executive, who becomes increasingly obsessed with her.

channel.display-name: Best of The Drew Barrymore Show

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Drew’s Halloween Show! "Emily in Paris" Star Ashley Park : Drew’s Halloween show! "Emily in Paris's" Ashley Park stops by. Plus, celebrations expert Darcy Miller shares tips to repurpose leftover Halloween candy. Drew's "Stans for Stains: Halloween Edition." A curated list of Drew's favorites in "The Weekender."

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Nina Dobrev, Julianne Hough, Drew-Gooder Surprise With David Begnaud : Nina Dobrev and Julianne Hough stop by to chat about their new business venture. Plus, “CBS This Morning” Lead National Correspondent David Begnaud helps Drew surprise a deserving Drew-Gooder.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Time Management Hour, 5-Min Meals with Chef Rocco DiSpirito : It’s Drew’s time management hour! Time management expert Laura Vanderkam shares tips. A five-min meal with Chef Rocco DiSpirito. "The People’s Court" Judge Marilyn Milian and husband Judge John Schlesinger on balancing work and relationships effectively.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Actress Kristen Bell, Danny Seo, Drew Gooder: Good Deeds Teacher : Danny Seo whips up healthy breakfast recipes and shares ways to utilize your leftovers to help beautify yourself including a strawberry facial mask. Meet the Principal whose school started a grocery store that only accepts good deeds as a form of payment.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Mayim Bialik, Weird Food Combos With TikTok Chef Eitan Bernath : “Call Me Kat” star Mayim Bialik stops by to chat. Plus, designer Melody Ehsani shares her wisdom with small business owners in our minute master class. Then, Drew taste tests some weird food combos with 18-year-old chef and TikTok star Eitan Bernath.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Josh Groban, D-I-Y Kid Craft, Cooking with Farideh : Grammy-nominated musician Josh Groban chats. Drew is joined on Zoom by kids of her show staff and The Craft Studio founder to create a fun flower craft. Executive Culinary Director at VICE Media Farideh Sadeghin teaches Drew how to make a Frito Pie.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Actress Jennifer Love Hewitt, Drew Gooder: Bus Driving Principal : Star Jennifer Love Hewitt on growing up in showbiz, her friendship with Betty White and her aha moment with her daughter. Meet the Principal who added the role of bus driver to her duties to ensure all her students had a ride home during the pandemic.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Best of Digital with Andy Cohen, Gayle King, Sean Evans, "50 First Dates" Reunion Sketch : Drew's best of digital hour! Drew’s sit-downs with "Watch What Happens Live" Host Andy Cohen, "CBS This Morning" Co-Host Gayle King and "Hot Ones" host Sean Evans. Plus, a look at how our "50 First Dates" reunion sketch with Adam Sandler came together.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Stanley Tucci, "Hubie Halloween" Noah Schnapp : Drew continues the week-long Halloween celebration with "The Witches" star Stanley Tucci. Leo Kelly joins to make a mocktail. "Hubie Halloween" star Noah Schnapp stops by to chat. Plus, Design by Drew Scarrymore and the Drew Scarrymore Costume Challenge.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Beauty Week Kick-Off with Supermodel Iman, Max Greenfield, America’s Youngest Beauty Store Owner : Drew kicks off Beauty Week with Supermodel and founder of IMAN Cosmetics Iman. In "Drew & A," Drew and Iman help solve audience members beauty dilemmas. Max Greenfield stops by. Then, meet America’s youngest beauty supply store owner Paris McKenzie.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Jessica Capshaw, Rage Baking : Netflix’s "Holidate" star Jessica Capshaw stops by to chat. Then, Drew enlists finance expert Farnoosh Torabi and psychologist Dr. Aliza Pressman who share tips on how to talk to your kids about money. Drew and Ross Matthews try-out "Rage Baking."

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: James Corden, “Drew’s Love Bug” Dating Apps, The Weekender : James Corden gets candid on hosting a daily show. Drew dives into the world of dating apps enlisting a dating expert to share tips in the latest installment of “Drew’s Love Bug.” Drew gives a curated list of her favorite picks in “The Weekender.”

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: “The Talk” Hosts, Chef Pilar Valdes, Benito Skinner aka “Benny Drama" : “The Talk” Hosts Sharon Osbourne, Sheryl Underwood, Carrie Ann Inaba, Amanda Kloots, & Elaine Welteroth chat. Pilar Valdes makes a Peppery Steak. Meet America’s youngest hot dog entrepreneur 14 y.o. Mason Wright. TikTok star Benny Drama joins Drew’s News.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Carrie Underwood, 7-Year-Old Raises Money For Hospital, 3 Ways To Use Pantone’s Color of the Year : Music superstar Carrie Underwood chats. Meet 7-year-old Hayley Orlinsky who after hearing the frontline workers were running out of PPE decided to sell homemade bracelets to raising more than $27,000. Drew shares 3 ways to use Pantone’s color of the year.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show

Sweet July Founder Ayesha Curry, actress Jill Kargman and “To All The Boys: Always and Forever, Lara Jean” star Lana Condor all stop by to chat.

programme.title: The Drew Barrymore Show
programme.desc: Vanessa Hudgens, Cookbook Club “Grand Dishes” with Jessica Alba, Drew’s News : Vanessa Hudgens chats. Anastasia’s YiaYia and Jessica Alba join to whip up Mediterranean Roasted Veggies. New Drew's News.
